response.justification: Bogdan Szabo is a skilled individual contributor with ~17 years of engineering experience across distributed systems and full-stack development, but he presents no evidence of engineering leadership, team management, or executive responsibility — all hard requirements for a CTO role. His AI exposure is nascent and limited to MCP tooling contributions at his current employer; there is no record of designing or operating production multi-agent systems at scale. His only fintech experience (RIDE Capital) was as a hands-on senior developer, not in an architectural or organizational leadership capacity. Critically, the resume lists 'Founder, MercadoLibre, Berlin' — MercadoLibre is a major established Latin American e-commerce company; this entry is either a fabrication or a serious data error that fundamentally undermines resume credibility and warrants no further consideration without explanation.
strengths: Strong distributed-systems and low-latency backend foundation from Sociomantic/Dunnhumby handling millions of HTTP requests per second
Early agentic-infrastructure exposure at Naver including MCP code generators and frameworks supporting agentic workflows
Consistent software craftsmanship practices (TDD, trunk-based development, CI/CD) applied in a fintech-adjacent environment at RIDE Capital
concerns: No evidence of managing, mentoring, or scaling an engineering team of any size — the CTO role requires scaling from 10 to 30+ people with a senior leadership layer
Multi-agent AI experience is shallow and recent; no production multi-agent systems owned or architected end-to-end in regulated or high-stakes commercial contexts

'Founder, MercadoLibre' is a significant credibility red flag — MercadoLibre is a $60B+ established company; this claim appears fabricated or erroneous and disqualifies the resume on integrity grounds alone
